lede: A look at September 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Alisha Palmowski and Michał Pazdan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2019 Albania earthquake
events.desc: A 5.6 Mw earthquake shakes the Albanian port of Durrës. Forty-nine people are injured in the capital, Tirana.

events.title: Defense of Marriage Act
events.desc: The Defense of Marriage Act is passed by the United States Congress.

events.title: 1993 Sukhumi airliner attacks
events.desc: A Transair Georgian Airlines Tu-134 is shot down by a missile in the Black Sea near Sokhumi, Georgia.

events.title: North American XB-70 Valkyrie
events.desc: The North American XB-70 Valkyrie, the world's fastest bomber, makes its maiden flight from Palmdale, California.

events.title: 1938 New England hurricane
events.desc: The Great Hurricane of 1938 makes landfall on Long Island in New York. The death toll is estimated at 500–700 people.

events.title: J. R. R. Tolkien
events.desc: J.R.R. Tolkien's The Hobbit is published for the first time. (September 22 is celebrated by some fans as Hobbit Day, however.)

events.title: 1934 Muroto typhoon
events.desc: A large typhoon hits western Honshū, Japan, killing more than 3,000 people.

events.title: Anglo-Egyptian conquest of Sudan
events.desc: Anglo-Egyptian conquest of Sudan: British forces under the command of Horatio Kitchener take Dongola.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Benedict Arnold gives the British the plans to West Point.

events.title: Anglo-Norman invasion of Ireland
events.desc: Anglo-Norman invasion of Ireland: The Kingdom of Dublin falls to Anglo-Norman invaders.

births.name: Donald Glaser
births.line: American physicist and neurobiologist, Nobel Prize laureate (died 2013)

births.name: Henry Tingle Wilde
births.line: English chief officer on the RMS Titanic (died 1912)

deaths.text: Raquel Blandón, Guatemalan lawyer and activist, First Lady of Guatemala
Kofi Awoonor, Ghanaian author, poet, and diplomat
Henry de Montherlant, French essayist, novelist, and dramatist
Ivan Mazepa, Ukrainian statesman, Hetman of Zaporizhian Host

observances.text: Autumnal equinox observances in the Northern Hemisphere, vernal equinox observances in the Southern Hemisphere (see September 22): Spring Day (Argentina)
Christian feast day: Laurent-Joseph-Marius Imbert (one of the Korean Martyrs)
Christian feast day: Matthew the Evangelist (Western Church)
Christian feast day: Nativity of the Theotokos (Eastern Orthodox Church, Julian calendar)
